steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Textual Evidence question. The claim is that the narrator "prefers her hometown to other places she has visited." The correct quotation must do two things: praise the hometown AND explicitly compare it to other places — both elements are required.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "There is always something fresh, something to be traced or discovered, something particularly to be remembered.": praises the hometown but makes no comparison to other places visited. ✗
  • Choice B — "Two large rivers join just below the village at the head of tide-water, and these, with the great inflow from the sea, make a magnificent stream, bordered by lovely green fields that slope gently to long lines of willows at the water's edge.": describes the hometown's scenery but contains no comparison to other places. ✗
  • Choice C — "I have had a good deal of journeying in my life, and taken great delight in it, but I have never taken greater delight than in my rides and drives and tramps and voyages within the borders of my native town.": explicitly contrasts "journeying in my life" with experiences in "my native town," proving the hometown is preferred over other places. ✓
  • Choice D — "There is never-ending pleasure in making one's self familiar with such a region.": praises the region but makes no comparison to other places the narrator has visited. ✗
C is correct.
